intent.feature_concept: HTTP client request bar: color-coded method dropdown, variable-highlighting URL input with unknown-var flagging against active env, and Send/Save/Share actions with keyboard shortcuts; reads/writes active tab RequestSpec
intent.topic: HTTP client request bar: color-coded method dropdown, variable-highlighting URL input with unknown-var flagging against active env, and Send/Save/Share actions with keyboard shortcuts; reads/writes active tab RequestSpec
intent.scope_summary: RequestBar UI for the active request tab. (1) Method selector: dropdown over GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD, each method color-coded via the 005 method-pill [data-mstyle] convention; reuses the 001 Dropdown molecule. (2) URL field: plain text input bound to RequestSpec.url (no var-highlight editor — that is epic D). (3) Actions: Send (disabled when url empty; fires a send-intent callback, NO HTTP here), Save (writes via the 004 dirty-flag + markClean path), Share (rendered token-styled in final layout position but wired to disabled/no-op stub — real share deferred). (4) Keyboard: ⌘↵ = Send, ⌘S = Save. Reads/writes only method+url of the active tab RequestSpec; does not touch auth.
intent.verbatim_prompt: The request bar of an HTTP client: a method dropdown (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD, color-coded by method), a URL input that highlights {{variable}} tokens and flags unknown ones against the active environment's valid-vars set, and Send / Save / Share actions. Send is blocked when the URL is empty and triggers the send flow; ⌘↵ sends, ⌘S saves. Reads and writes the active request tab's RequestSpec (method + url) already present in the renderer's tab store. Use the existing Dropdown molecule from specs/001-ui-primitives (Radix-backed, controlled via open/onOpenChange) for the method selector rather than a new dropdown. HTTP execution and the response are separate tasks. Visual reference: design/reference.html — look and behavior only, never reproduce its markup; it carries generated cruft (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, a tweaks-panel) that must not enter the build. Rebuild with semantic class names from each component's role and its tag's function. Colors come from design/tokens.json.

constraints.content: tokens.css semantic classes, zero inline styles; no design-export JSX/cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, tweaks-panel). Reuse 001 Dropdown (Radix) for method selector — no second overlay lib; preserve its a11y; icons from existing Icon atom. Method color via 005 [data-mstyle] convention (consume, don't reinvent). Writes only via 004 dirty-flag + markClean; do not touch auth. Renderer process only; React 19 + TS strict; semantic class names from role/tag function. @renderer alias for imports; new files per atomic structure (RequestBar is an organism in its domain home per 006 placement rule); no sibling-organism imports. Co-located tests under __tests__/ (.test.tsx Vitest + .ct.tsx Playwright CT) matching established convention. Shortcut registration must not leak across tabs/unmount: bind to active tab, clean up listeners; ⌘S must not trigger browser/native save (preventDefault). No new runtime deps (Dropdown/Icon/state all exist); no network, no disk, no telemetry. Single-line URL input only (no textarea/multiline); horizontal scroll, no reflow. a11y: method selector keyboard-operable via Radix Dropdown semantics; Send disabled-state accessible (aria-disabled / disabled).

Out of scope: HTTP execution / actually sending the request (separate task). Response rendering (separate task). {{variable}} highlighting + unknown-var flagging + interpolation -> epic D. No inline-highlight editor (CodeMirror/contenteditable overlay). Auth editing (discriminated-union placeholder, later task). Real Share behavior (cURL/link/export). No new dropdown/overlay library. URL parsing/validation/autocomplete/history-suggestions — url is a plain bound string (no scheme-check, no past-url dropdown). Query-param/KV editor (separate task). Body, headers, request sub-tabs (separate tasks). Durable persistence to disk/collections — Save persists the in-memory RequestSpec via 004 dirty/markClean only; persistence-port is its own task. Tab create/close/reorder/rename — owned by existing tab strip (002); this feature only reads/writes the focused tab's RequestSpec. Send-intent callback consumer (HTTP engine) is out — this feature fires the intent only.
§2.2 / §5.2 requestSpec is a pure data module — no component imports
§4 Mutate state only through store actions; tabsStore owns tab lifecycle
§4 Never use inline styles; prefer design tokens

affected_areas.impact: read active tab method+url; REQUIRES a new store action to mutate spec.method/spec.url + set dirty (none exists; markClean clears only) — candidate for reuse-by-extension
method+url field types this feature binds to
consume existing .method.* color classes via data-mstyle
organisms are FLAT (TabBar.tsx/Sidebar.tsx, only shell/ nested) — RequestBar lands flat organisms/RequestBar.tsx, NOT organisms/request/; mounts into Shell panes.request slot, composed at App root
internal — holds active tab + RequestSpec; exposes read (tabs/activeTabId) + markClean, BUT no spec-edit/dirty-set action (gap)
internal — RequestSpec type {method,url,name,params,headers,body,auth}; method+url are the fields this feature edits
internal — reuse for the method selector; no second overlay lib needed
internal — per-method color classes already exist; consume via .method {METHOD} class, data-mstyle set on <html> by Shell
internal — sibling organism showing the exact zustand subscribe/memoize pattern RequestBar should mirror; lives flat at organisms/
internal — established global ⌘B shortcut pattern; reuse shape for ⌘↵ Send / ⌘S Save (preventDefault stops native save)

risks.risk: TabsState has no spec-edit/dirty-set mutator; a new store action (e.g. updateActiveSpec/setMethod+setUrl that patches the active tab spec and flips dirty=true) is required

open_questions.question: user-supplied placement guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): active request tab RequestSpec (method+url) already present in renderer tab store — request bar reads/writes it

design_options.name: Generic spec-patch store action
design_options.shape: Add updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>) to tabsStore: shallow-merge the patch into the active tab's spec and set dirty=true in one action. RequestBar subscribes to active method+url via per-field selectors and calls updateActiveSpec({method}) / updateActiveSpec({url}); Save calls markClean; Send fires a send-intent callback.
pros: single new mutator, minimal store surface
extensible to future fields (headers/body/params) with zero new actions
one dirty-set site keeps the dirty invariant centralized
matches the existing zustand action-only mutation pattern (§4)
cons: patch is untyped-ish (Partial) — needs a guard to reject unknown keys
slightly less explicit than named field actions at call sites

design_options.name: Field-specific store actions
design_options.shape: Add setActiveMethod(method) and setActiveUrl(url) to tabsStore; each writes its field into the active tab spec and sets dirty=true. RequestBar calls the matching action per control.
cons: store surface grows one action per editable field — does not scale to headers/body later
two dirty-set sites to keep consistent
more boilerplate for the same effect

design_options.name: Local draft + commit-on-action
design_options.shape: RequestBar holds local method/url state and only writes to the store on blur/Send/Save (deferred commit), rather than writing on every keystroke.
cons: breaks reactive dirty-on-edit — tab would not show dirty while typing
loses live per-tab sync; external/tab-switch changes can clobber the draft
contradicts the stated edge-case + success criteria (edit marks dirty immediately)
diverges from the established per-field-selector store pattern

build_vs_buy.build_path: Assemble RequestBar from existing internal primitives (Dropdown molecule, RequestSpec model, method-color tokens, Shell shortcut/selector patterns) plus one new tabsStore spec-patch action. No new runtime dependency.
build_vs_buy.buy_path: Adopt an external request-bar / form component library or a generic dropdown+input kit.
build_vs_buy.reasoning: Every building block already exists in-repo and the only missing piece is a ~10-line store action; an external lib would duplicate the Dropdown molecule, fight the zustand store pattern and tokens.css, and violate the no-second-overlay-lib constraint. Build is strictly cheaper and lower-risk.

cited_canonical_patterns.reference: HTTP method color convention (GET green/POST orange/DELETE red)
cited_canonical_patterns.relevance: industry-standard request-bar UX (Postman/Insomnia); already encoded in project tokens.css .method.* — no new work
canonical ⌘Enter/⌘S handling; matches project Shell Effect 4; preventDefault stops native save

plan_seeds.recommended_option_rationale: Extends the existing internal:src/renderer/src/lib/tabsStore.ts store — which currently covers active-tab read + markClean but NOT spec-edit/dirty-set — with one generic updateActiveSpec(patch) mutator. This is the minimal, KISS/DRY-aligned closure of the only real gap: it keeps all mutation behind a store action (§4), centralizes the dirty-set, scales to later request fields without new actions, and lets RequestBar stay a thin reactive consumer mirroring the TabBar selector pattern. Tradeoff accepted: a small key-allow-list guard on the patch to preserve type safety at the boundary.

discovery_block.fit_rationale: Almost pure assembly of existing primitives — Dropdown molecule, RequestSpec model, method-color tokens (data-mstyle), and the Shell global-shortcut + per-field-selector patterns all exist and are directly reusable. One real integration gap: the tab store has a clean-side API (markClean) but NO action to edit the active tab's spec.method/url and set dirty — that write mutator must be added (Medium). The user's placement belief (organisms/request/ domain nesting) is slightly off: organisms are flat, so RequestBar lands flat alongside TabBar and mounts into the Shell panes.request slot. No new runtime deps, no second overlay lib.

fit_assessments.user_expected: writes go through the existing dirty-flag + markClean path already in the store
fit_assessments.reality: store exposes read (tabs/activeTabId) + markClean (clear dirty) ONLY; NO action mutates spec.method/spec.url and NO action sets dirty=true — the edit/dirty-set write path does not exist and must be added
blockers: TabsState has no spec-edit/dirty-set mutator; a new store action (e.g. updateActiveSpec/setMethod+setUrl that patches the active tab spec and flips dirty=true) is required

fit_assessments.user_expected: RequestBar nested under organisms/request/ (domain home), same as TabBar; follow TabBar if it moved
fit_assessments.reality: organisms are FLAT (organisms/TabBar.tsx, organisms/Sidebar.tsx); only organisms/shell/ is nested; no organisms/request/ folder exists and TabBar never moved. RequestBar lands flat organisms/RequestBar.tsx matching siblings; mounts into the Shell panes.request slot composed at the App root

fit_assessments.user_expected: reuse existing 001 Radix Dropdown for method selector
fit_assessments.reality: molecules/Dropdown.tsx exports Dropdown/DropdownItem/DropdownLabel/DropdownSeparator, Radix-backed, controlled via open/onOpenChange — directly reusable
.method.* per-method classes live in tokens.css; data-mstyle is set on <html> by Shell Effect 1 from settingsStore — already wired, RequestBar just applies .method {METHOD}
RequestSpec = {method,url,name,params,headers,body,auth}; method+url top-level, confirmed via makeBlankRequest

fit_assessments.user_expected: RequestBar nested under organisms/request/ (domain home), same as TabBar; follow TabBar if it moved
fit_assessments.reality: Constitution §2.2: single-domain components live in organisms/<domain>/ but the subfolder is created ONLY at >=2 components (TabBar/Sidebar are currently flat domain singletons, no organisms/request/ exists). So the belief is a DEFERRED decision, not wrong: if RequestBar makes a 'request' domain reach >=2 with TabBar, create organisms/request/ and move TabBar in; otherwise RequestBar lands flat as another singleton. The /plan stage owns this call. Either way it mounts into the Shell panes.request slot (composed at App root).
blockers: placement is a /plan decision (flat singleton vs organisms/request/ at >=2-component trigger, which would relocate TabBar)

functional_scope.value: RequestBar UI for the active request tab. (1) Method selector: dropdown over GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD, each method color-coded via the 005 method-pill [data-mstyle] convention; reuses the 001 Dropdown molecule. (2) URL field: plain text input bound to RequestSpec.url (no var-highlight editor — that is epic D). (3) Actions: Send (disabled when url empty; fires a send-intent callback, NO HTTP here), Save (writes via the 004 dirty-flag + markClean path), Share (rendered token-styled in final layout position but wired to disabled/no-op stub — real share deferred). (4) Keyboard: ⌘↵ = Send, ⌘S = Save. Reads/writes only method+url of the active tab RequestSpec; does not touch auth.

users.value: Single local developer/API-tester operating the mintEnvoy desktop renderer. Keyboard-driven (⌘↵ Send, ⌘S Save), mouse for method selection + Share. No multi-user, no auth/identity, no remote/collab surface.

inputs_outputs.value: In: reactive read — subscribe to the active tab's RequestSpec so external/tab-switch changes re-render the bar; active-tab identity (which tab to read/write) is also an input; user edits (url text, method choice); action triggers (Send/Save/Share clicks, ⌘↵/⌘S). Out: writes (method+url) target the active tab only via 004 dirty-flag (set on change, cleared by Save->markClean); no write on pure focus/blur or method-menu open without a change. Send-intent emitted only when url is non-empty-after-trim, carrying nothing beyond the active tab's identity / current RequestSpec (method+url); no HTTP, no response data, no disk I/O. Share emits nothing (no-op stub).

integration_points.value: BELIEF (Phase 2/CBM reconciles actual paths + exports): RequestBar = request-domain organism under organisms/request/ (006 domain-nesting rule; same domain home as TabBar, NOT flat organisms/ root) — confirm the request domain folder exists post-006 and follow TabBar if it moved there. Consumes the existing Dropdown molecule + Icon atom (001) — bind real module paths via CBM, not assumed filenames. Reads/writes the active tab's RequestSpec through the existing tab store + RequestSpec model/state-machine (004); dirty + markClean are that module's API — reference by role, CBM resolves actual store/model files + exported symbols. Method pill color via 005 [data-mstyle] convention. Mounted by the shell/layout organism hosting the request pane (003 app-shell region) — confirm exact mount point + how active-tab context reaches it. @renderer alias; no sibling-organism imports (constitution §2.2).

constraints.value: tokens.css semantic classes, zero inline styles; no design-export JSX/cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, tweaks-panel). Reuse 001 Dropdown (Radix) for method selector — no second overlay lib; preserve its a11y; icons from existing Icon atom. Method color via 005 [data-mstyle] convention (consume, don't reinvent). Writes only via 004 dirty-flag + markClean; do not touch auth. Renderer process only; React 19 + TS strict; semantic class names from role/tag function. @renderer alias for imports; new files per atomic structure (RequestBar is an organism in its domain home per 006 placement rule); no sibling-organism imports. Co-located tests under __tests__/ (.test.tsx Vitest + .ct.tsx Playwright CT) matching established convention. Shortcut registration must not leak across tabs/unmount: bind to active tab, clean up listeners; ⌘S must not trigger browser/native save (preventDefault). No new runtime deps (Dropdown/Icon/state all exist); no network, no disk, no telemetry. Single-line URL input only (no textarea/multiline); horizontal scroll, no reflow. a11y: method selector keyboard-operable via Radix Dropdown semantics; Send disabled-state accessible (aria-disabled / disabled).

non_goals.value: HTTP execution / actually sending the request (separate task). Response rendering (separate task). {{variable}} highlighting + unknown-var flagging + interpolation -> epic D. No inline-highlight editor (CodeMirror/contenteditable overlay). Auth editing (discriminated-union placeholder, later task). Real Share behavior (cURL/link/export). No new dropdown/overlay library. URL parsing/validation/autocomplete/history-suggestions — url is a plain bound string (no scheme-check, no past-url dropdown). Query-param/KV editor (separate task). Body, headers, request sub-tabs (separate tasks). Durable persistence to disk/collections — Save persists the in-memory RequestSpec via 004 dirty/markClean only; persistence-port is its own task. Tab create/close/reorder/rename — owned by existing tab strip (002); this feature only reads/writes the focused tab's RequestSpec. Send-intent callback consumer (HTTP engine) is out — this feature fires the intent only.

success_criteria.value: Layout [method ▾][URL][Send] matches Postman/Insomnia pattern + design reference visually; semantic classes bound to tokens.css, zero inline styles; Share button in final slot disabled/no-op. RequestSpec read: bar renders active tab method+url; tab switch swaps values with no cross-tab bleed. RequestSpec write: url edit + method change write back to active tab only; method pill uses 005 [data-mstyle]. Dirty integration: any method/url edit marks tab dirty via 004; Save (when dirty) persists + markClean clears flag; Save on non-dirty = no-op. Send: disabled when url empty-after-trim, else fires send-intent callback (no HTTP). Shortcuts: ⌘↵ Send + ⌘S Save act on active tab globally; Save respects dirty; trim guard applies to ⌘↵. Overflow: long url scrolls horizontally, method pill+Send do not reflow. Method selector reuses 001 Dropdown (no second overlay lib); icons use existing Icon atom. Tests green: unit (RequestSpec read/write, trim guard, dirty/markClean, method-switch independence) + CT (layout, disabled Send, shortcut behavior, per-tab isolation); typecheck+lint+build pass.

edge_cases.value: Whitespace-only URL: trim before the empty-guard; empty-after-trim blocks Send (same disabled state as empty); never send a whitespace URL. ⌘S on a non-dirty tab: no-op (no write, no markClean, no toast) — Save acts only when dirty. Shortcut focus scope: ⌘↵ (Send) and ⌘S (Save) act on the active tab globally regardless of focus (target the active tab RequestSpec); URL-edit shortcuts stay local to the input. Rapid method switching mid-edit: method and url are independent RequestSpec fields — switching method never touches the url buffer or caret; each method change is its own committed dirty edit, no debounce coupling. Very long URL: single-line input, no wrap, horizontal scroll within the field; layout (method pill/Send) must not reflow or grow; no truncation of stored value, visual scroll only.

references: Postman/Insomnia request-bar layout ([method ▾][URL][Send]) — visual reference only, build from scratch
Dropdown molecule + Icon atom (specs/001-ui-primitives) — reuse Radix Dropdown for method selector, no second overlay lib
RequestSpec / working-tabs state machine (specs/004-working-tabs-state-machine) — method+url are RequestSpec fields, writes via dirty-flag + markClean
method-pill color convention (specs/005-tab-bar-visual-fidelity) — runtime [data-mstyle] attribute, consume it
tokens.css semantic classes, zero inline styles

gaps.description: user-supplied placement guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): active request tab RequestSpec (method+url) already present in renderer tab store — request bar reads/writes it
